claim_boundary: This protocol is executable research design. It does not register a scientific prediction, authorize a sealed measurement, report a result, or establish that HSWM already learns durable weights, transfers between agents, or outperforms matched baselines.
question: Can a typed hypergraph of frozen LLM functions use one shared HSWM state for query-conditioned inference and then convert independently measured outcomes into causal, durable semantic-weight changes that improve later frozen-agent behavior under equal compute?

execution_model.node_equation: y_i = f_i(x_i, R_i(H_t, q); model_i, prompt_i)
execution_model.forward_path: QueryEnvelopeV1 -> QF_QUERY_COMPILER -> QueryPlanV1 -> BF_BOND_PROPOSER -> BondProposalV1 -> frozen HSWM readout -> AF_ANSWER_SYNTHESIZER -> AnswerEnvelopeV1
execution_model.learning_path: external OutcomeEnvelopeV1 -> used-bond EligibilityTraceV1 -> query-conditioned fast BondProposalV1 -> fresh/retention/canary evaluation -> repeated-effect SparseSemanticDeltaV1 -> immutable slow-weight candidate -> approval and CAS activation
durable_state: typed function registry and port topology
provenance, evaluation, activation, and removal receipts
execution_model.evaluator: An external sealed evaluator owns outcome and promotion evidence; none of the three LLM functions may score its own scientific success.

stages.purpose: Build the complete query-by-candidate observable component substrate required before any bond learner can be fitted.
forbidden_actions: register a positive prediction from Gate-0 output
required_inputs: guarded CUDA device with batch size 128
exit_evidence: all three role roots reopen and replay

stages.purpose: Establish that typed LLM functions and their composition, rather than extra calls or tokens, cause an inference benefit.
permitted_actions: implement the three-role registry and typed ports
record every call, prompt, response, model, token count, and port hash
develop equal-budget flat and vector baselines
run role-removal and role-shuffle development controls
forbidden_actions: let a role read test gold or external outcomes
give the typed arm more calls or tokens
call three prompts a network without role ablations
let an LLM role emit the scientific verdict
exit_evidence: role registry and deterministic orchestration replay
token parity within the registered tolerance

stages.purpose: Test whether external outcome bound to actually used bonds can cause a query-conditioned fast change and a separately validated durable slow-weight change.
permitted_actions: fit one low-capacity A/B-renaming-invariant fast bond scorer on accepted learner views
propose but do not directly activate SparseSemanticDeltaV1
evaluate fresh, retention, canary, replay, and removal arms
forbidden_actions: reuse the rejected A/B/MERGED router search
use query ID, edge ID embeddings, answer strings, test gold, or hop labels
jointly change weights, router, and topology
tune after sealed outcomes are opened
required_inputs: server-registered prediction receipt before sealed measurement
exit_evidence: random-credit, shuffled-eligibility, static-update, and no-promotion controls do not explain the effect

stages.purpose: Test whether Agent A can change shared HSWM state and thereby improve a frozen Agent B on unseen work without transcript leakage or parameter updates.
permitted_actions: write only through the validated HSWM mutation path
freeze Agent B model, prompt, tools, readout, and budget
compare removal, flat memory, vector memory, and transcript-only arms
forbidden_actions: Agent A transcript visibility in the transfer arm
topology mutation before transfer causality is established
exit_evidence: frozen Agent B fresh unseen gain
causal rewrite removal erases the gain
flat and vector memory matched-budget comparison

llm_functions.role: Compile a user query into typed evidence requirements; never answer the query.
llm_functions.model_policy: Frozen model revision shared across every experimental arm unless a preregistered model-ablation says otherwise.
llm_functions.prompt: You are QF_QUERY_COMPILER, a typed function in HSWM. Convert the QueryEnvelopeV1 into one JSON QueryPlanV1 object. Decompose only what is required to answer, name evidence types rather than answer strings, preserve request_id exactly, and respect the supplied budget. Do not answer the user, select evidence, modify HSWM, predict experiment success, issue a scientific verdict, or inspect outcome labels. If the request is underspecified or unsafe, set abstain=true and state the missing constraint. Output JSON only with exactly these field types: request_id string; objectives array of unique non-empty strings; required_evidence_types array of unique non-empty strings; constraints array of unique non-empty strings; abstain boolean. Do not return an object or string where an array is required.
llm_functions.abstention: abstain=true when evidence requirements cannot be formed without inventing facts or exceeding the budget.

llm_functions.role: Map a typed query plan and observable candidate components to a bounded query-conditioned bond potential; never see gold outcomes.
llm_functions.model_policy: Frozen model revision; output is a volatile proposal and cannot alter slow weights.
reads: observable score, provenance, and structural components
llm_functions.prompt: You are BF_BOND_PROPOSER, a typed function in HSWM. Given one QueryPlanV1 and an opaque fixed candidate table, return one JSON BondProposalV1. Preserve request_id exactly. Rank only supplied candidate IDs and assign each selected candidate a finite numeric potential <= 0, where 0 is least suppressed. Use only supplied observable score, provenance, and structural components. Do not use answer strings, query IDs as features, edge-ID embeddings, hop labels, gold labels, private entity surfaces, prior test outcomes, or your own confidence as promotion authority. Do not answer the query, mutate slow HSWM state, or issue a scientific verdict. Output JSON only with exactly these field types: request_id string; ordered_bond_ids array of unique supplied bond-ID strings; bond_potentials object mapping every ordered bond ID to one finite number <= 0 and containing no other keys; evidence_refs array of unique supplied evidence-ID strings corresponding to selected bonds; abstain boolean. When abstain=true, return empty ordered_bond_ids, bond_potentials, and evidence_refs.
llm_functions.abstention: abstain=true and emit no potentials when the observable components do not support a stable distinction.

llm_functions.role: Produce an evidence-bound answer from the frozen selected payload; never select new bonds or judge the experiment.
llm_functions.model_policy: Frozen model revision with the same answer budget in every arm.
llm_functions.prompt: You are AF_ANSWER_SYNTHESIZER, a typed function in HSWM. Answer only from the selected evidence in AnswerContextV1. Preserve request_id exactly and cite only supplied evidence IDs. Do not retrieve additional material, rerank candidates, alter HSWM, infer hidden outcome labels, or issue a scientific verdict. If the evidence is insufficient or conflicting, set abstain=true and describe the uncertainty without inventing support. Output JSON only with exactly these field types: request_id string; answer string containing only the shortest answer span, or an empty string when abstaining; supporting_evidence_ids array of unique supplied evidence-ID strings actually used; uncertainty string; abstain boolean. Do not return an object or array where a string is required.
llm_functions.abstention: abstain=true when selected evidence cannot support the answer.

budget_contract.call_parity: Every F1 arm, including removal and shuffle controls, executes exactly three physical model calls; a removed role is replaced by a schema-preserving null call whose usage is still charged.
budget_contract.token_parity: Register one total input-plus-output token cap before measurement. Pad or truncate non-semantic control context without exposing labels; report both allowed and consumed tokens per item and arm.
budget_contract.retrieval_parity: All arms receive the same candidate universe and candidate-count budget.
budget_contract.state_parity: Mutable bytes, persistent items, and serialized-state capacity are inventoried and capped for shared, flat, and vector arms.
budget_contract.cost_ledger: Record model revision, prompt hash, input/output hash, tokens, latency, retries, cache status, candidate count, and persistent bytes for every call.

evaluation.split_contract: Use query- and gold-component-disjoint development, validation, and sealed test splits. P1v5 confirmation uses a fresh unopened regime after model, features, lambda, sparsity, abstention, and promotion rules are frozen.

primary_metrics.F1: paired sealed task success of typed network minus flat single-LLM workflow under equal calls and tokens
primary_metrics.P1V5: component-bootstrap 95% lower bound of fresh recall@10 delta after validated slow promotion versus frozen MERGED
primary_metrics.P2: paired fresh unseen frozen-Agent-B task success after Agent-A HSWM write minus no-A-information baseline

promotion_gates: fresh overall recall@10 component-bootstrap 95% lower bound > 0
exact replay and equal-budget checks pass
causal removal erases the claimed durable effect
independent evaluator and activation receipt agree
evaluation.reporting: Report every arm, seed, regime, exclusion, abstention, retry, token, cost, and failed gate. No aggregate success may hide a failed retention or control gate.

conclusion_rules: INVALID: any preregistration, hash, split, leakage, exact-replay, evaluator-independence, or equal-budget contract fails.
F1_SUPPORTED_NARROW: the typed network beats the flat and vector matched-budget arms, while at least one role-removal and role-shuffle control loses the effect, on the registered sealed scope.
FAST_ONLY: query-conditioned fast bonds pass but slow promotion or removal causality fails; retain attention only and do not claim durable semantic-weight learning.
P1V5_SUPPORTED_NARROW: all fresh, minimum-delta, retention, negative-control, replay, slow-promotion, and removal gates pass.
P2_SUPPORTED_NARROW: frozen Agent B improves on fresh unseen work only through the accepted HSWM write, with transcript hidden, no parameter update, no exact-cache hit, and removal erasing the gain.
REJECTED_OR_NARROWED: every valid result not satisfying the relevant support rule; do not retune the sealed run.
kill_conditions: Gate-0 full packs remove the previously observed oracle room.
The learned fast bond scorer cannot produce positive confidence intervals on both development datasets.
Private-surface removal erases the apparent benefit.
Slow distillation violates retention or its removal does not erase the effect.
Flat or vector memory matches or exceeds HSWM under equal compute and capacity.
Role ablations do not reduce F1 performance, indicating a multi-prompt workflow rather than function topology.
Agent B requires Agent A transcript, a parameter update, or an exact-query cache hit.
Three diversified candidates fail the preregistered vector gate; terminate the branch as SATURATED rather than tuning indefinitely.
